<path>/home/azureuser/backport-testing-opencode-with-tools/.opencode-backport-worktrees/row-0018-bf171d8341da/tensorflow/core/kernels/string_ngrams_op.cc</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /* Copyright 2019 The TensorFlow Authors. All Rights Reserved.
2: 
3: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
4: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
5: You may obtain a copy of the License at
6: 
7:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
8: 
9: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
10: distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
11: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
12: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
13: limitations under the License.
14: ==============================================================================*/
15: 
16: #include <locale>
17: #include <string>
18: 
19: #include "absl/strings/ascii.h"
20: #include "absl/strings/str_cat.h"
21: #include "tensorflow/core/framework/op_kernel.h"
22: #include "tensorflow/core/lib/core/errors.h"
23: 
24: namespace tensorflow {
25: namespace text {
26: 
27: namespace {
28: template <typename SPLITS_TYPE>
29: class StringNGramsOp : public tensorflow::OpKernel {
30:  public:
31:   explicit StringNGramsOp(tensorflow::OpKernelConstruction* context)
32:       : tensorflow::OpKernel(context) {
33:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->GetAttr("separator", &separator_));
34:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->GetAttr("ngram_widths", &ngram_widths_));
35:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->GetAttr("left_pad", &left_pad_));
36:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->GetAttr("right_pad", &right_pad_));
37:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->GetAttr("pad_width", &pad_width_));
38:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->GetAttr("preserve_short_sequences",
39:                                              &preserve_short_));
40:   }
41: 
42:   int get_pad_width(const int ngram_width) const {
43:     // Ngrams can be padded with either a fixed pad width or a dynamic pad
44:     // width depending on the 'pad_width' arg, but in no case should the padding
45:     // ever be wider than 'ngram_width' - 1.
46:     return std::min(pad_width_ < 0 ? ngram_width - 1 : pad_width_,
47:                     ngram_width - 1);
48:   }
49: 
50:   int get_num_ngrams(const int length, const int ngram_width) const {
51:     int pad_width = get_pad_width(ngram_width);
52:     return std::max(0, ((length + 2 * pad_width) - ngram_width) + 1);
53:   }
54: 
55:   void Compute(tensorflow::OpKernelContext* context) override {
56:     const tensorflow::Tensor* data;
57:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->input("data", &data));
58:     const auto& input_data = data->flat<tstring>().data();
59: 
60:     const tensorflow::Tensor* splits;
61:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->input("data_splits", &splits));
62:     const auto& splits_vec = splits->flat<SPLITS_TYPE>();
63: 
64:     // Validate that the splits are valid indices into data
65:     const int input_data_size = data->flat<tstring>().size();
66:     const int splits_vec_size = splits_vec.size();
67:     for (int i = 0; i < splits_vec_size; ++i) {
68:       bool valid_splits = splits_vec(i) >= 0;
69:       valid_splits = valid_splits && (splits_vec(i) <= input_data_size);
70:       OP_REQUIRES(
71:           context, valid_splits,
72:           errors::InvalidArgument("Invalid split value ", splits_vec(i),
73:                                   ", must be in [0,", input_data_size, "]"));
74:     }
75: 
76:     // If there is no data or size, return an empty RT.
77:     if (data->flat<tstring>().size() == 0 || splits_vec.size() == 0) {
78:       tensorflow::Tensor* empty;
79:       OP_REQUIRES_OK(context,
80:                      context->allocate_output(0, data->shape(), &empty));
81:       OP_REQUIRES_OK(context,
82:                      context->allocate_output(1, splits->shape(), &empty));
83:       return;
84:     }
85: 
86:     int num_batch_items = splits_vec.size() - 1;
87:     tensorflow::Tensor* ngrams_splits;
88:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(
89:         context, context->allocate_output(1, splits->shape(), &ngrams_splits));
90:     auto ngrams_splits_data = ngrams_splits->flat<SPLITS_TYPE>().data();
91: 
92:     ngrams_splits_data[0] = 0;
93:     for (int i = 1; i <= num_batch_items; ++i) {
94:       int length = splits_vec(i) - splits_vec(i - 1);
95:       int num_ngrams = 0;
96:       for (int ngram_width : ngram_widths_)
97:         num_ngrams += get_num_ngrams(length, ngram_width);
98:       if (preserve_short_ && length > 0 && num_ngrams == 0) {
99:         num_ngrams = 1;
100:       }
101:       ngrams_splits_data[i] = ngrams_splits_data[i - 1] + num_ngrams;
102:     }
103: 
104:     tensorflow::Tensor* ngrams;
105:     OP_REQUIRES_OK(
106:         context,
107:         context->allocate_output(
108:             0, TensorShape({ngrams_splits_data[num_batch_items]}), &ngrams));
109:     auto ngrams_data = ngrams->flat<tstring>().data();
110: 
111:     for (int i = 0; i < num_batch_items; ++i) {
112:       auto data_start = &input_data[splits_vec(i)];
113:       int output_start_idx = ngrams_splits_data[i];
114:       for (int ngram_width : ngram_widths_) {
115:         auto output_start = &ngrams_data[output_start_idx];
116:         int length = splits_vec(i + 1) - splits_vec(i);
117:         int num_ngrams = get_num_ngrams(length, ngram_width);
118:         CreateNgrams(data_start, output_start, num_ngrams, ngram_width);
119:         output_start_idx += num_ngrams;
120:       }
121:       // If we're preserving short sequences, check to see if no sequence was
122:       // generated by comparing the current output start idx to the original
123:       // one (ngram_splits_data). If no ngrams were generated, then they will
124:       // be equal (since we increment output_start_idx by num_ngrams every
125:       // time we create a set of ngrams.)
126:       if (preserve_short_ && output_start_idx == ngrams_splits_data[i]) {
127:         int data_length = splits_vec(i + 1) - splits_vec(i);
128:         // One legitimate reason to not have any ngrams when preserve_short_
129:         // is true is if the sequence itself is empty. In that case, move on.
130:         if (data_length == 0) {
131:           continue;
132:         }
133:         // We don't have to worry about dynamic padding sizes here: if padding
134:         // was dynamic, every sequence would have had sufficient padding to
135:         // generate at least one ngram.
136:         int ngram_width = data_length + 2 * pad_width_;
137:         auto output_start = &ngrams_data[output_start_idx];
138:         int num_ngrams = 1;
139:         CreateNgrams(data_start, output_start, num_ngrams, ngram_width);
140:       }
141:     }
142:   }
143: 
144:   void CreateNgrams(const tstring* data, tstring* output, int num_ngrams,
145:                     int ngram_width) const {
146:     for (int ngram_index = 0; ngram_index < num_ngrams; ++ngram_index) {
147:       int pad_width = get_pad_width(ngram_width);
148:       int left_padding = std::max(0, pad_width - ngram_index);
149:       int right_padding =
150:           std::max(0, pad_width - (num_ngrams - (ngram_index + 1)));
151:       int num_tokens = ngram_width - (left_padding + right_padding);
152:       int data_start_index = left_padding > 0 ? 0 : ngram_index - pad_width;
153: 
154:       // Calculate the total expected size of the ngram so we can reserve the
155:       // correct amount of space in the string.
156:       int ngram_size = 0;
157:       // Size of the left padding.
158:       ngram_size += left_padding * left_pad_.length();
159:       // Size of the tokens.
160:       for (int n = 0; n < num_tokens; ++n) {
161:         ngram_size += data[data_start_index + n].length();
162:       }
163:       // Size of the right padding.
164:       ngram_size += right_padding * right_pad_.length();
165:       // Size of the separators.
166:       int num_separators = left_padding + right_padding + num_tokens - 1;
167:       ngram_size += num_separators * separator_.length();
168: 
169:       // Build the ngram.
170:       tstring* ngram = &output[ngram_index];
171:       ngram->reserve(ngram_size);
172:       for (int n = 0; n < left_padding; ++n) {
173:         ngram->append(left_pad_);
174:         ngram->append(separator_);
175:       }
176:       for (int n = 0; n < num_tokens - 1; ++n) {
177:         ngram->append(data[data_start_index + n]);
178:         ngram->append(separator_);
179:       }
180:       ngram->append(data[data_start_index + num_tokens - 1]);
181:       for (int n = 0; n < right_padding; ++n) {
182:         ngram->append(separator_);
183:         ngram->append(right_pad_);
184:       }
185: 
186:       // In debug mode only: validate that we've reserved enough space for the
187:       // ngram.
188:       DCHECK_EQ(ngram_size, ngram->size());
189:     }
190:   }
191: 
192:   string separator_;
193:   string left_pad_;
194:   string right_pad_;
195:   bool use_pad_;
196:   bool extend_pad_;
197:   bool preserve_short_;
198: 
199:   std::vector<int> ngram_widths_;
200:   int pad_width_;
201: };
202: 
203: }  // namespace
204: REGISTER_KERNEL_BUILDER(Name("StringNGrams")
205:                             .Device(tensorflow::DEVICE_CPU)
206:                             .TypeConstraint<int32>("Tsplits"),
207:                         StringNGramsOp<int32>);
208: REGISTER_KERNEL_BUILDER(Name("StringNGrams")
209:                             .Device(tensorflow::DEVICE_CPU)
210:                             .TypeConstraint<int64>("Tsplits"),
211:                         StringNGramsOp<int64>);
212: 
213: }  // namespace text
214: }  // namespace tensorflow

(End of file - total 214 lines)
</content>

500:   MakeOp("|", {5}, "LP", "RP", 1, false);
501:   // Batch items are:
502:   // 0: "a"
503:   // 1: "b", "c", "d"
504:   // 2: "e", "f"
505:   AddInputFromArray<tstring>(TensorShape({6}), {"a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"});
506:   AddInputFromArray<int64>(TensorShape({4}), {0, 1, 4, 6});
507:   TF_ASSERT_OK(RunOpKernel());
508: 
509:   std::vector<tstring> expected_values({"LP|b|c|d|RP"});

511: 
512:   assert_string_equal(expected_values, *GetOutput(0));
513:   assert_int64_equal(expected_splits, *GetOutput(1));
514: }
515: 
516: TEST_F(NgramKernelTest, TestSinglyPaddedUnigrams) {
517:   MakeOp("|", {1}, "LP", "RP", 1, false);
518:   // Batch items are:
519:   // 0: "a", "b", "c", "d"
520:   // 1: "e", "f"
521:   AddInputFromArray<tstring>(TensorShape({6}), {"a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"});
522:   AddInputFromArray<int64>(TensorShape({3}), {0, 4, 6});
523:   TF_ASSERT_OK(RunOpKernel());
524: 
525:   std::vector<tstring> expected_values({"a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"});
526:   std::vector<int64> expected_splits({0, 4, 6});
527: 
528:   assert_string_equal(expected_values, *GetOutput(0));
529:   assert_int64_equal(expected_splits, *GetOutput(1));
530: }
531: 
532: TEST_F(NgramKernelTest, TestEmptyInput) {
533:   MakeOp("|", {1}, "LP", "RP", 3, false);
534:   AddInputFromArray<tstring>(TensorShape({0}), {});
535:   AddInputFromArray<int64>(TensorShape({0}), {});
536:   TF_ASSERT_OK(RunOpKernel());
537: 
538:   std::vector<tstring> expected_values({});
539:   std::vector<int64> expected_splits({});
540: 
541:   assert_string_equal(expected_values, *GetOutput(0));
542:   assert_int64_equal(expected_splits, *GetOutput(1));
543: }
544: 
545: TEST_F(NgramKernelTest, ShapeFn) {
546:   ShapeInferenceTestOp op("StringNGrams");
547:   INFER_OK(op, "?;?", "[?];[?]");
548:   INFER_OK(op, "[1];?", "[?];[?]");
549:   INFER_OK(op, "[1];[2]", "[?];in1");
550:   INFER_ERROR("Shape must be rank 1 but is rank 0", op, "[];?");
551:   INFER_ERROR("Shape must be rank 1 but is rank 0", op, "?;[]");
552: }
553: 
554: }  // namespace text
555: }  // namespace tensorflow

(End of file - total 555 lines)
</content>

144:   void CreateNgrams(const tstring* data, tstring* output, int num_ngrams,
145:                     int ngram_width) const {
146:     for (int ngram_index = 0; ngram_index < num_ngrams; ++ngram_index) {

148:       int left_padding = std::max(0, pad_width - ngram_index);
149:       int right_padding =

151:       int num_tokens = ngram_width - (left_padding + right_padding);
152:       int data_start_index = left_padding > 0 ? 0 : ngram_index - pad_width;
153: 
154:       // Calculate the total expected size of the ngram so we can reserve the
155:       // correct amount of space in the string.
156:       int ngram_size = 0;
157:       // Size of the left padding.
158:       ngram_size += left_padding * left_pad_.length();
159:       // Size of the tokens.
160:       for (int n = 0; n < num_tokens; ++n) {
161:         ngram_size += data[data_start_index + n].length();

163:       // Size of the right padding.
164:       ngram_size += right_padding * right_pad_.length();
165:       // Size of the separators.
166:       int num_separators = left_padding + right_padding + num_tokens - 1;
167:       ngram_size += num_separators * separator_.length();
168: 
169:       // Build the ngram.

171:       ngram->reserve(ngram_size);

176:       // Only output first num_tokens - 1 pairs of data and separator
